channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
item.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make 'poop talk' OK
item.description: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.content:encoded: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: These fans are boycotting the World Cup. Will they make it a bust?
item.description: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry.
item.content:encoded:
Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry. 

(Image credit: Ty Malugani)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: The Iraq vet redefining mindfulness, one bourbon at a time
item.description: Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."
item.content:encoded:
Bottom Shelf: How a Forgotten Brand of Bourbon Saved One Man's Life.'/>

Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."

(Image credit: Dustin Franz for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: After lackluster tour turnout, Turning Point wraps things up with excited crowd in Idaho
item.description: The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.
item.content:encoded:
The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.

(Image credit: Saige Miller)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
item.description: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.
item.content:encoded:
By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
item.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.
item.content:encoded:
In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

(Image credit: Ariel Schalit)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: The U.S. economy shows resilience despite the war with Iran
item.description: The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
item.description: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.
item.content:encoded:
Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.

(Image credit: Joan Mateu Parra)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Taxpayers might pay millions more to fight wildfires as jet fuel skyrockets
item.description: The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.
item.content:encoded:
The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Austin Amestoy
